source_packet.title: Trust the Batch, On- or Off-Policy: Adaptive Policy Optimization for RL Post-Training
source_packet.audience: Readers who know policy gradients and want to understand the paper's on-policy and off-policy formulation.
source_packet.central_question: Can the current batch quantify behavior-policy mismatch and set both policy-gradient weighting and off-policy regularization without a fixed clipping factor?

section_policy.sequence_rule: Begin with the paper's motivating question, establish the prior objective and terminology before the mechanism, connect the mechanism to original code, then separate experimental evidence from limitations.

formula_code_links.role: computes e_B from valid per-token ratios across ranks
forms the ratio, calls calculate_ess, caps rho, adds (1-e_B) behavioral KL, and reports ess_factor

example.label: One math prompt in synchronous P3O training
example.input: A prompt is sampled and produces several tokenized completions.
example.output: The completion contributes to a P3O update, metrics, and the next rollout-policy version.

nodes.label: Prompt shard
nodes.detail: A concrete math prompt enters collect_rollouts.

nodes.label: Generate and score completions
nodes.detail: The rollout engine returns response tokens, rewards, z-scores, masks, and old log-probabilities.

nodes.label: Store one trajectory
nodes.detail: ReplayBuffer rejects empty or over-length responses and stores aligned per-token tensors.

nodes.label: Collate a training micro-batch
nodes.detail: Sequences are padded and action-token masks preserve which positions contribute to training.

nodes.label: Current-policy forward pass
nodes.detail: P3O recomputes current log-probabilities for the same completion tokens.

nodes.label: Compute ratio and normalized ESS
nodes.detail: Valid-token ratios are aggregated across ranks into e_B.

nodes.label: Assemble P3O loss
nodes.detail: e_B caps detached rho and weights the behavioral KL term; metrics retain ess_factor.

nodes.label: Backpropagate and update
nodes.detail: DeepSpeed performs backward and the optimizer boundary advances the policy.

nodes.label: Sync weights to rollout engines
nodes.detail: The next epoch generates data with the new policy version.

experiment_pipeline.label: Generate rollouts
experiment_pipeline.detail: vary clipping factor, sampling temperature, or rollout precision

experiment_pipeline.label: Compute GRPO/P3O update
experiment_pipeline.detail: same model family; objective is the controlled factor

equation_coverage.intent: Establish the estimator before changing the sampling distribution.
Show the fixed clipping factor used by PPO-style objectives.
Move the expectation to behavior-policy samples and define the per-token policy ratio.
Separate the off-policy regularizer from the fixed clipping mechanism before P3O couples them through e_B.
Expose how Eq. 4 acts on the two advantage signs.
equation_coverage.fold_reason: They expand the fixed-clipping objective by advantage sign but do not introduce a new control variable; the related-work transition keeps Eq. 4 visible and links this algebra to the appendix.

equation_coverage.intent: Compute the batch statistic that measures concentration of policy ratios.
Use e_B in both the score-function weight and behavioral KL coefficient.
Separate behavior-policy mismatch from within-epoch policy drift and expose the potential issue discussed by the authors.

finding_coverage.question: Does performance depend on choosing a clipping factor?
finding_coverage.setting: GRPO with ε ∈ {0.2, 0.4, 0.6} versus one P3O objective
finding_coverage.takeaway: The paper reports sensitivity across fixed clip choices while P3O does not require a clip-ratio choice.

finding_coverage.question: How does sampling-temperature mismatch affect training?
finding_coverage.setting: Qwen3-4B with rollout temperature T=0.6 versus T=1.2
finding_coverage.takeaway: The paper reports P3O remains robust while GRPO degrades at the larger mismatch.

finding_coverage.question: Does train–rollout precision mismatch cause late collapse?
finding_coverage.setting: BF16 Train + FP8 Rollout at iterations 15 and 30
finding_coverage.takeaway: Table 7 reports GRPO falling from 0.499 to 0.029 while P3O changes from 0.478 to 0.529.

finding_coverage.question: Does the two-anchor extension remain stable under temperature mismatch?
finding_coverage.takeaway: The appendix reports the extension can be stable at default temperature but becomes unstable at T=1.2, preserving an important claim boundary.

formula_showcase.title: Eqs. 2, 5–8 · move samples to π_b
formula_showcase.focus: The expectation changes sampling policy; the per-token ratio and behavioral KL appear for distinct reasons.

formula_showcase.title: Eq. 11 · measure effective support
formula_showcase.focus: The token count stays fixed while ratio concentration lowers normalized ESS.

formula_showcase.title: Eq. 12 · couple two controls
formula_showcase.focus: One e_B lowers the score cap and raises the behavioral KL coefficient.

paper_excerpts.text: The paper names a trust-region concern and an off-policy concern, argues that fixed clipping conflates them, and asks the current batch to measure behavior-policy mismatch through normalized effective sample size. Preserve ownership: PPO contributes fixed clipping; Fakoor et al.'s original P3O already sets its off-policy cap c to ESS and mixture coefficient lambda to one minus ESS; FeynRL formulates the corresponding normalized, per-token P3O objective for large-model post-training.
Equation 11 defines normalized ESS from per-token policy ratios. Equation 12 uses e_B in the score-function weight and the off-policy regularizer of Policy-on Policy-off Policy Optimization.
The experiments test clipping factor, sampling temperature, and BF16 Train plus FP8 Rollout. Table 7 reports exact held-out pass@1 values, including the late precision-mismatch result.

code_notes.text: The implementation computes normalized ESS on valid tokens, caps rho at ESS, detaches rho in the policy term, and weights behavioral KL by one minus ESS.

agent_input.claim_policy: Use the paper's exact terms. Attribute fixed clipping to PPO, the ESS-controlled on/off-policy structure to original P3O, and the normalized per-token formulation to FeynRL. Use exact numbers only with equation or table locators. Label figure readings as authors' reported findings. Do not rename P3O components or invent motivational slogans.

concept_graph.thesis: The paper measures behavior-policy mismatch with normalized effective sample size and uses that batch value in the original P3O objective for large-model post-training.

nodes.label: Trust-region concern
nodes.summary: Each gradient update should not move the policy too far.

nodes.label: Off-policy concern
nodes.summary: Behavior-policy data should influence an update only when the data remain reliable.

nodes.label: Fixed clipping
nodes.summary: A clipping range chosen before training is used in PPO-style and GRPO-style objectives.

nodes.label: Normalized effective sample size
nodes.summary: Equation 11 summarizes concentration of the current batch's per-token policy ratios.

nodes.label: Policy-on Policy-off Policy Optimization
nodes.summary: Equation 12 uses e_B in the score-function weight and the off-policy regularizer.

edges.teaching_point: Fixed clipping is commonly used to limit an update.
The paper argues that the same fixed range is also asked to handle data mismatch.
The current batch, rather than a pre-committed range, provides e_B.
The batch value appears in both terms of Eq. 12.
Section 4 compares the fixed-clipping baselines with P3O across controlled shifts.

unresolved: The paper notes that one ESS can conflate behavior-policy mismatch with within-epoch proximal drift.

lesson_plan.title: Trust the Batch, On- or Off-Policy: Adaptive Policy Optimization for RL Post-Training
lesson_plan.promise: Follow the paper's trust-region concern and off-policy concern into normalized ESS, the adopted P3O objective, its implementation, and the experiments designed to test it.

sections.title: Trust-region concern and off-policy concern
sections.intent: Separate two concerns that a fixed clipping factor treats as one: limiting each update and correcting mismatch between the behavior and current policies.
sections.question: Why does the paper argue that a fixed clipping factor is inadequate?
sections.learning_goal: Distinguish the two concerns using the paper's own terms.
sections.misconception: A fixed clipping range directly measures the mismatch in the current batch.
sections.summary: The clipping factor is fixed before training, while behavior-policy mismatch changes with the batch.

sections.title: Fixed clipping and prior policy-optimization objectives
sections.intent: Trace how the score-function estimator becomes an importance-weighted objective and then a fixed-clipped objective, so the precise change made by P3O is visible.
sections.question: Which ideas precede the paper's use of P3O for large-model post-training?
sections.learning_goal: Place TRPO, PPO, GRPO, off-policy correction, and original P3O in the paper's stated lineage.
sections.misconception: P3O is introduced here without a prior objective or off-policy literature.
sections.summary: The paper adopts the original Policy-on Policy-off Policy Optimization objective and contrasts its batch-adaptive control with fixed clipping.

sections.title: Effective sample size of policy ratios
sections.intent: Introduce the batch statistic that P3O will use to replace a preset clipping factor and control off-policy regularization.
sections.question: Why does Eq. 11 appear before the P3O objective?
sections.learning_goal: Read normalized ESS as the paper's batch-computed measure of behavior-policy mismatch.
sections.misconception: ESS is necessary because the physical number of tokens changes.
sections.summary: For a fixed number of tokens, ESS falls when a few policy ratios dominate the weighted estimate.

sections.title: Policy-on Policy-off Policy Optimization
sections.intent: Show where the same batch-computed e_B enters the score-function weight and behavioral KL term of the adopted P3O objective.
sections.question: How does e_B enter both terms of Eq. 12?
sections.learning_goal: Map the score-function weight and off-policy regularizer to confirmed repository lines.
sections.misconception: P3O merely substitutes ESS for PPO's upper clip bound.
sections.summary: The batch value caps the score-function weight and scales the KL regularizer, while every valid token still contributes.

sections.title: Experimental findings
sections.intent: Test the claimed removal of clip-factor tuning and robustness to behavior-policy mismatch across temperature and precision changes.
sections.question: Which controlled changes are the experiments designed to test?
sections.learning_goal: Connect clipping factor, sampling temperature, and rollout precision to the paper's reported findings.
sections.misconception: One held-out bar is the complete evidence for the method.
sections.summary: Section 4 tests three mismatch regimes; Table 7 connects the training behavior to exact held-out pass@1 values.

sections.title: Potential issue and claim boundary
sections.intent: Examine the paper's two-anchor extension and preserve the boundary between tested regimes and a general stability claim.
sections.question: What does one batch-level ESS combine, and what does the evidence not establish?
sections.learning_goal: Separate the paper's potential issue from extrapolated claims.
sections.misconception: A single ESS necessarily identifies every source of mismatch or guarantees stability in all regimes.
sections.summary: One ESS can conflate behavior drift with within-epoch proximal drift; the evidence remains tied to the tested models and regimes.

blocks.label: Paper premise
blocks.question: A clipping factor is fixed before training. How should policy optimization respond when behavior-policy mismatch changes from batch to batch?
blocks.context: The paper separates a trust-region concern from an off-policy concern and attributes fragility to using fixed clipping for both.

columns.label: Trust-region concern
columns.question: How far should one gradient update move the policy?
columns.answer: The update should not move the policy too far.

columns.label: Off-policy concern
columns.question: When should behavior-policy data influence the update?
columns.answer: Only when the data remain reliable for the current policy.

blocks.heading: Why the paper moves beyond fixed clipping
paragraphs: A fixed range ρ_t ∈ [1−ε_l, 1+ε_h] is selected before training. It is therefore not a measurement of the current batch. The paper's intent is to compute a batch-dependent quantity and use it in an objective that handles both on-policy and off-policy data.

blocks.title: Which paper introduced each control—and why FeynRL reuses P3O

stages.intent: Use a fixed ε to limit how much a sampled ratio contributes.
stages.change: Open question for this paper: ε is selected before the current batch reveals policy mismatch.

stages.formula: J_on + λ J_off(c) + KL, with c=ESS and λ=1−ESS
stages.intent: Interleave on-policy and off-policy gradients while effective sample size controls off-policy influence.
stages.change: Inheritance: P3O already couples an importance-weight cap and mixing coefficient through ESS.

stages.paper: Trust the Batch, On- or Off-Policy
stages.intent: Evaluate an update using samples from the behavior policy.
stages.change: Adaptation: express the behavior correction at every generated token in language-model post-training.

stages.equation: FeynRL Eqs. 11–12 · normalized ESS and P3O
stages.paper: Trust the Batch, On- or Off-Policy
stages.intent: Let the current batch set both controls.
stages.change: The paper uses normalized e_B in both min{ρ_t,e_B} and (1−e_B) in one token-level objective.

folded.reason: They expand Eq. 4 by advantage sign but introduce no new control variable; the appendix retains the algebra.

blocks.title: Watch ownership and adaptation, not just symbol morphing
blocks.intro: Each held frame names the source paper, the control it introduced, and the unresolved question carried into the next step.
blocks.observation: PPO contributes fixed clipping; original P3O contributes ESS-controlled on/off-policy mixing; FeynRL specializes that control to per-token language-model ratios.
blocks.consequence: The animation distinguishes inherited P3O structure from FeynRL's post-training formulation.

blocks.title: What changes when the samples come from π_b?
blocks.intro: The animation keeps the score-function estimator fixed, then changes only the sampling distribution from the current policy to the behavior policy.
blocks.observation: Moving the expectation to y∼π_b introduces the per-token ratio ρ_t; the behavioral KL is shown as a separate relation to π_b.
blocks.consequence: This is the bridge from the on-policy premise to the two quantities that Eq. 12 later controls with e_B.

blocks.heading: The paper's stated intent
paragraphs: The objective is not presented as a renamed PPO clip. The paper adopts the original P3O objective for large-model post-training so that the same form can use on-policy and off-policy data without adding a new clipping hyperparameter.

blocks.title: Further reading in the paper's lineage

items.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
items.relation: Clipped surrogate objective used as a proximal baseline.

items.title: P3O: Policy-on Policy-off Policy Optimization
items.relation: The original P3O objective adopted by the paper.

items.title: DeepSeekMath: Pushing the Limits of Mathematical Reasoning in Open Language Models
IMPALA: Scalable Distributed Deep-RL with Importance Weighted Actor-Learner Architectures
items.relation: Off-policy actor-learner correction and policy-lag context.

blocks.label: Why Eq. 11 is necessary
blocks.question: Does the current batch have broad effective support, or do a few policy ratios dominate its estimate?
blocks.context: Normalized ESS is the paper's batch-computed measure of behavior-policy mismatch. It supplies e_B before Eq. 12 uses that value.

blocks.title: Same token count, different normalized ESS
blocks.note: The token count is unchanged. ESS falls because the second moment grows when a few ratios dominate.
blocks.claim_label: Eq. 11 with a derived numeric fixture

blocks.title: What changes when policy ratios concentrate?
blocks.intro: Both states contain the same four valid tokens. Only the distribution of policy ratios changes.
blocks.observation: When one ratio dominates, the second moment in the denominator grows and normalized ESS falls from 1.000 to 0.292.
blocks.consequence: This is why Eq. 11 is not a substitute for batch size: it measures effective support in the current batch.

blocks.prompt: If all four policy ratios doubled but stayed equal, would normalized ESS fall?
blocks.answer: No. Equation 11 is scale-normalized. Equal ratios still distribute weight uniformly, so normalized ESS remains 1; concentration, not the common scale, lowers it.

blocks.label: What Eq. 12 must do
blocks.question: Once e_B measures effective support, how should the objective respond to a batch that is less reliable for the current policy?
blocks.context: P3O uses the same batch statistic in two places: it limits the detached score-function weight and increases behavioral KL regularization.

blocks.formula: L_P3O = −sg(min{ρ_t,e_B}) log π_θ A_t + (1−e_B) KL(π_θ || π_b)

steps.label: Compute e_B
steps.meaning: Equation 11 measures behavior-policy mismatch in the current batch.

steps.label: Score-function weight
steps.meaning: The paper caps the detached score-function weight with e_B.

steps.label: Off-policy regularizer
steps.meaning: The KL coefficient grows as the batch value falls.

blocks.title: One batch value controls two responses
blocks.intro: Equation 12 does not use e_B in only one place. The same value enters the detached score-function weight and the behavioral KL coefficient.
blocks.observation: As e_B falls, min{ρ_t,e_B} imposes a lower cap while 1−e_B applies a larger KL weight.
blocks.consequence: P3O couples how strongly the batch drives the score term with how strongly the update is regularized toward the behavior policy.

blocks.symbol: Equation 11 and Equation 12 in the repository

blocks.prompt: Why is P3O not simply PPO with e_B substituted for an upper clipping bound?
blocks.answer: Equation 12 also scales the behavioral KL term by 1−e_B, and the score term uses a detached min{ρ_t,e_B} weight. The batch statistic therefore couples two controls rather than replacing one constant.

blocks.question: Does performance depend on choosing a clipping factor?
blocks.setting: GRPO with ε ∈ {0.2, 0.4, 0.6}; P3O is evaluated once without a clip-ratio choice.
blocks.evidence: Figure 1 compares training trajectories; Table 7 provides held-out values. No curve samples are invented.
blocks.takeaway: The paper reports sensitivity across fixed clipping settings, which is the hyperparameter dependence P3O is designed to remove.

blocks.title: Collapse three clip choices into the held-out readout
blocks.intro: The clip begins with ε∈{0.2,0.4,0.6}, then replaces those choices with the exact Table 7 aggregate on the same 4K-token AMC evaluation.
blocks.observation: The reported GRPO clip average is 0.381±0.195; P3O is 0.493 and does not select a clip ratio.
blocks.consequence: The animation connects the method-level hyperparameter question to a specific held-out metric without inventing Figure 1 curve samples.

blocks.question: How does sampling-temperature mismatch affect training?
blocks.setting: Qwen3-4B rollouts at T=0.6 and T=1.2.
blocks.evidence: Figure 2 is summarized qualitatively because its curve has not been digitized.
blocks.takeaway: The authors report that P3O remains robust while GRPO degrades at the larger mismatch.

blocks.question: Does BF16 Train + FP8 Rollout cause late collapse?
blocks.setting: Held-out evaluation at iterations 15 and 30 under precision mismatch.
blocks.metric: Average pass@1 across the Table 7 benchmarks.
blocks.takeaway: The exact late-stage values connect the reported training collapse to retained held-out performance.

blocks.title: One controlled setting change, two trajectories
blocks.intro: The clip fixes the precision-mismatch setting and metric, then moves from iteration 15 to iteration 30.
blocks.observation: The bars begin close at iteration 15; by iteration 30 the GRPO average is 0.029 and the P3O average is 0.529.
blocks.consequence: Motion represents the experimental trajectory: only the iteration changes while metric and setting stay fixed.

blocks.title: Findings organized by the paper's experimental questions

items.label: Clipping factor
items.finding: Across the compared clipping settings, the paper reports substantial sensitivity for GRPO while P3O is evaluated once without choosing a clipping factor.

items.label: Sampling temperature
items.finding: The paper reports that changing rollout temperature introduces off-policy data and that P3O remains stable in the shown runs.

items.label: BF16 Train + FP8 Rollout
items.finding: Table 7 reports 0.029 for GRPO and 0.529 for P3O at iteration 30 in the shown precision-mismatch setting.

items.label: One ESS
items.detail: The paper notes that one effective sample size can conflate behavior-policy mismatch with within-epoch proximal drift.

items.label: Aggregation level
items.detail: The formulation computes a batch-level value; it does not separately diagnose every sequence or token subgroup.

items.label: Evidence boundary
items.detail: The experiments cover the paper's selected models, datasets, temperatures, clipping factors, rollout lengths, and precision settings.

items.label: No universal guarantee
items.detail: The reported stability and held-out values do not establish performance for every model or distribution shift.

blocks.prompt: Why is ESS needed if the batch size is already known?
blocks.answer: Batch size counts tokens. Equation 11 measures how evenly the policy-ratio weight is distributed across those tokens; the count can stay fixed while effective support changes.

animation_plan.purpose: Show the objective lineage as a sequence of changed controls rather than another paragraph.
animation_plan.before_state: fixed clipping and prior policy-optimization objectives are named in HTML
animation_plan.after_state: the reader can locate the batch-adaptive control introduced by P3O

animation_plan.purpose: Make the sampling-distribution change and its two mathematical consequences visible.
animation_plan.after_state: the behavior ratio and behavioral KL have distinct roles

animation_plan.purpose: Hold token count fixed while ratio concentration changes normalized ESS.
animation_plan.before_state: the reader has compared two numeric fixtures
animation_plan.after_state: ESS is understood as effective support, not batch size

animation_plan.purpose: Couple the two Eq. 12 controls through the same batch statistic.
animation_plan.after_state: the reader sees why P3O is not a clip-bound substitution

animation_plan.purpose: Collapse the clipping-factor sweep into one exact held-out comparison.
animation_plan.before_state: the experimental question and metric are fixed
animation_plan.after_state: the exact Table 7 comparison can be interpreted without axis ambiguity

animation_plan.purpose: Show the reported training change under the precision-mismatch setting.
animation_plan.before_state: the setting and evidence kind are explicit
animation_plan.after_state: the trajectory is tied back to the paper's reported finding

appendix_entries.title: Source provenance
appendix_entries.body: Paper OpenReview FOGhcI4iRk / arXiv:2605.12380v1, PDF SHA-256 19b66e…15e. Code revision dfe85351…5752. Every new media file is hash checked.

appendix_entries.title: Related-work reading order
appendix_entries.body: Read TRPO for the trust-region constraint, PPO for the clipped surrogate, DeepSeekMath for GRPO in mathematical reasoning, original P3O for the on-policy/off-policy objective, and IMPALA for policy-lag correction context.

appendix_entries.title: Normalized ESS
appendix_entries.body: Equation 11 is mean(ρ)² / mean(ρ²), equivalently (Σρ)² / (nΣρ²). For nonnegative ratios it reaches 1 for uniform weights and decreases as weight concentrates.

appendix_entries.title: Formula, animation, and code map
appendix_entries.body: The new Manim source lives in scenes/explainer_pipeline_native.py. It animates Eq. 11, Eq. 12, and exact repository operations in one scene; the runtime never asks a coding agent to author Python.

appendix_entries.title: Results reading guide
appendix_entries.body: Bars use exact Table 7 values. Figure statements remain labeled as the authors' reported findings; curves are not digitized into invented samples.

appendix_entries.title: Potential issue and open questions
appendix_entries.body: The main conceptual limitation is whether one batch-level ESS can adequately distinguish different kinds of policy mismatch. The website preserves that issue instead of presenting P3O as assumption-free.

manim_compatibility.statement: The formula primitives have observed render evidence on Manim 0.19.0 and 0.20.1. No minimum-version compatibility claim is made.

manim_nodes.label: manim.BarChart
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Axis ranges and labels must be fixed before transforming between charts.
limitations: Negative values require an explicit y-range.

manim_nodes.label: manim.MathTex
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Use MathTex for paper equations; isolate semantic substrings before applying Write, Indicate, or TransformMatchingTex.
limitations: Requires a working LaTeX toolchain in the render environment.

manim_nodes.label: manim.Transform
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Source and target objects occupy compatible scene regions.
limitations: Semantic token matching needs TransformMatchingTex or authored grouping.

manim_nodes.label: manim.TransformMatchingTex
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Keep shared mathematical tokens spatially continuous so the reader sees exactly what a method adds, removes, or replaces.
limitations: Requires semantic TeX grouping when equivalent expressions use different tokenization.

manim_nodes.label: scenes.explainer_primitives.EssTradeoffGauge
manim_nodes.animation_contract: The ratio histogram, ESS gauge, cap, and KL weight update from the same batch values.
limitations: The composite is rendered and callable; its formula-to-scene mapping remains candidate until a reviewed teaching scene promotes it.

manim_nodes.label: scenes.explainer_primitives.FormulaCodeBridge
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Keep exact repository text and paper formula fragments side by side; let the website carry the prose interpretation.
limitations: The composite assumes short reviewed code excerpts; it is not a source-code browser.

manim_nodes.label: scenes.formula_explainer_scene.OperationWalk
manim_nodes.animation_contract: Every operation chip shows semantic operation text plus a non-color origin badge.
limitations: The first renderer lays out a topological sequence rather than arbitrary branches.

code_nodes.role: computes e_B from valid per-token ratios across ranks
forms the ratio, calls calculate_ess, caps rho, adds (1-e_B) behavioral KL, and reports ess_factor

formula_code_edges.role: computes e_B from valid per-token ratios across ranks
forms the ratio, calls calculate_ess, caps rho, adds (1-e_B) behavioral KL, and reports ess_factor

example.label: One math prompt in synchronous P3O training
example.input: A prompt is sampled and produces several tokenized completions.
example.output: The completion contributes to a P3O update, metrics, and the next rollout-policy version.

dag_nodes.label: Prompt shard
dag_nodes.detail: A concrete math prompt enters collect_rollouts.

dag_nodes.label: Generate and score completions
dag_nodes.detail: The rollout engine returns response tokens, rewards, z-scores, masks, and old log-probabilities.

dag_nodes.label: Store one trajectory
dag_nodes.detail: ReplayBuffer rejects empty or over-length responses and stores aligned per-token tensors.

dag_nodes.label: Collate a training micro-batch
dag_nodes.detail: Sequences are padded and action-token masks preserve which positions contribute to training.

dag_nodes.label: Current-policy forward pass
dag_nodes.detail: P3O recomputes current log-probabilities for the same completion tokens.

dag_nodes.label: Compute ratio and normalized ESS
dag_nodes.detail: Valid-token ratios are aggregated across ranks into e_B.

dag_nodes.label: Assemble P3O loss
dag_nodes.detail: e_B caps detached rho and weights the behavioral KL term; metrics retain ess_factor.

dag_nodes.label: Backpropagate and update
dag_nodes.detail: DeepSpeed performs backward and the optimizer boundary advances the policy.

dag_nodes.label: Sync weights to rollout engines
dag_nodes.detail: The next epoch generates data with the new policy version.

experiment_pipeline.label: Generate rollouts
experiment_pipeline.detail: vary clipping factor, sampling temperature, or rollout precision

experiment_pipeline.label: Compute GRPO/P3O update
experiment_pipeline.detail: same model family; objective is the controlled factor
